---
title: "AWK Commands Analysis using R"
author: "KUSHAl CHHAJED"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
```

---

##  2]  Preparation of DATASET




```{r}


data("diamonds", package = "ggplot2")
file_path <- file.path(getwd(), "diamonds.csv")
fwrite(diamonds[1:5, ], file_path)
```

``` 
# As mentioned in the description of easy task that " setwd("~/Downloads/")

fwrite(x = diamonds[1:5,], file = "diamonds.csv") "
and as we can see i have written in this format " file_path <- file.path(getwd(), "diamonds.csv")
fwrite(diamonds[1:5, ], file_path) " the reason is, it was creating an error to locate the path(file) ! 
```
---

##  3] Command 1


## Command 1

```{r}
fread(cmd = "awk -F ',' '{print}' 'diamonds.csv'")
```



##  Explanation Part

```
### Explanation of Command 1
            
The AWK command  is:

`awk -F ',' '{print}' diamonds.csv`

- `awk`--> A text-processing tool used to read and manipulate files line by line.
- `-F ','`-> Specifies that the input file is comma-separated.
- `{print}`--> Prints each line as it is (equivalent to `{print $0}`).
- `diamonds.csv`:- Input file.

                __ Functionality__
This command reads the entire CSV file and prints every row without any modification.

                   __ Result__
The output is identical to the original dataset.
```

## Command 2

```{r}
fread(cmd = "awk -F ',' \"NR>1 {print $1, $2, $7/$1}\" diamonds.csv", fill = TRUE)
```
```
 The ABOVE code is slightly different from the actual code due to WINDOWS-COMPATIBLE VERSION
 ( fread(cmd = "awk -F ',' '{if(NR > 1) print $1, $2, $7/$1}' 'diamonds.csv'", fill = T) )

```

##  Explanation Part

```
### Explanation of Command 2

The AWK command  is:

`awk -F ',' '{if(NR > 1) print $1, $2, $7/$1}' diamonds.csv`

- `-F ','`: Specifies comma as the field separator.
- `NR`: Represents the current row number.
- `NR > 1`: Skips the header row.
- `$1`: First column (carat).
- `$2`: Second column (cut).
- `$7`: Seventh column (price).
- `$7/$1`: Calculates price per carat.
- `print $1, $2, $7/$1`: Outputs selected columns and computed value.

                 __ Functionality __ 
This command skips the header, selects specific columns, and computes a new value (price per carat).

                    __Result__ 
A transformed dataset with three columns: carat, cut, and price per carat.
```
## 4] Comparison of Commands
```
-------------------------------------------
| Feature         | Command 1 | Command 2 |
|                 |           |           |
| Reads full data | Yes       | No        |    -----> just to understand the comparison
| Skips header    | No        | Yes       |
| Selects columns | No        | Yes       |
| Performs calculation| No    | Yes       |
-------------------------------------------
```
